# Citation bracket fixture

HTTP/2 was developed by the IETF working group[\[1\]](http://mock/#cite_1) based on Google's earlier SPDY protocol[\[2\]](http://mock/#cite_2). The standardisation document is RFC 7540, later obsoleted by RFC 9113.

This fixture verifies that bracket-escaping inside link text — which CommonMark requires to prevent nested-link parser errors — is preserved. The escaped form `[\[1\]](#cite_1)` is spec-correct and must not be "fixed". A second paragraph keeps Readability happy with enough substantive prose to score the article positively.

This section is intentionally short.
